swaps: Use half the tastemaker + a pinch of homemade spice mix to cut sodium
Stir in a boiled egg or a handful of paneer cubes for protein
Add grated carrot, peas, and spinach for fibre and volume
Use whole wheat/millet noodles if available
maggi.macroNote: Roughly same great taste, protein goes from ~8g to ~18g, and you add real vegetables instead of just noodles.

swaps: Mix multigrain atta (wheat + jowar/bajra) instead of only refined wheat
Cook with a teaspoon of ghee brushed on, not shallow-fried in oil
Load the stuffing with extra veggies or paneer for protein
Pair with curd instead of butter on top
paratha.macroNote: Similar comfort-food taste with meaningfully less oil and more fibre + protein per piece.

swaps: Use Greek yogurt + a small amount of cream instead of heavy cream/butter-only base
Keep the classic spice blend and tomato base exactly the same
Use skinless chicken breast or thigh over fried chicken pieces
Finish with a teaspoon of butter for flavour instead of a ladle
butter chicken.macroNote: Same rich, iconic flavour with a noticeably better fat profile and slightly higher protein.

swaps: Keep the whole spices and dum-cooking method for authentic flavour
Use a leaner protein cut (chicken breast/thigh, or extra paneer for veg)
Slightly reduce the ghee/oil layer between rice and masala
Serve with raita and a side salad to add volume without extra calories
biryani.macroNote: Same aromatic biryani experience with a better protein-to-carb-to-fat ratio.
